title: Against Oblivion
content:
Cities drowned in olden time
        Keep, they say, a magic chime
        Rolling up from far below
        When the moon-led waters flow.

        So within me, ocean deep,
        Lies a sunken world asleep.
        Lest its bells forget to ring,
        Memory! set the tide a-swing!
meaning:
Henry John Newbolt, Sir's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Against Oblivion"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
